description:
src/lib.rs:215 ChainedCredential::get_token awaits each source serially with no per-source timeout. ManagedIdentityCredential calls IMDS at 169.254.169.254 with the SDK's default timeout (which can be on the order of tens of seconds depending on platform).

Trigger: developer machine or CI runner that is not on Azure, using credential=chained (the default).

Bad behavior: every fetch pays the full IMDS timeout on the first call, before falling through to the developer credential. Local dev experience is poor and CI pipelines stall.

Fix options: (a) wrap the inner get_token call in tokio::time::timeout with a small budget (e.g. 2s) for non-final sources; (b) document that 'managed-identity' mode should be explicit in production and 'developer' should be explicit on dev machines so chained should rarely be used in practice; (c) probe for a metadata endpoint reachability hint before attempting MI.
title: Chained credential has no per-source timeout; IMDS hang in non-Azure env
